title: aparine borealis
intro: Aparine borealis, also known as sticky willow or stickyweed, is a perennial plant in the knotweed family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, due to its high content of apigenin and other flavonoids. The health benefits of Aparine borealis include reducing fever, relieving joint pain and swelling, and treating wounds and skin conditions. Aparine borealis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical application, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine.
